issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The base font size is set to 12px, which makes the default text size (1rem) too small for readability. This affects users with visual impairments and elderly users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 172: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text harder to read and distinguish. This affects users with dyslexia and low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 198: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: Negative letter-spacing reduces the space between characters, causing them to overlap or blur together. This significantly impairs readability for all users, especially those with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 199: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Line-height below 1.5
issuesFound.explanation: A line-height of 1 is too cramped for paragraph text, making it difficult for users to track lines. WCAG recommends a line-height of at least 1.5 for body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: line-height: 1.5;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed
issuesFound.explanation: Removing underlines from links makes them indistinguishable from surrounding text except by color. This fails to meet accessibility standards for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 225: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global focus-visible style removes the default browser outline without providing a visible alternative. This makes it impossible for keyboard users to know which element is currently focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: outline: 2px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the focus outline for non-keyboard interactions. While intended to reduce visual noise, it can hide focus from users who use a mix of input methods or certain assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 351: outline: 2px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The current implementation of the visually-hidden utility uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the content visible on the screen. This defeats the purpose of a screen-reader-only class.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class uses a transform transition for its active state without a prefers-reduced-motion override. Rapid movement or scaling can trigger motion sickness in sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary

issuesFound.explanation: The body element uses a negative word-spacing value of -0.2em. This reduces the space between words, making text harder to distinguish and read for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: Underlines are removed from all anchor elements globally. Without underlines, links within blocks of text rely solely on color to be identified, which is a failure for users with color vision deficiency.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline to none without defining a visible replacement style like a solid outline or box-shadow. This prevents keyboard users from knowing which element currently has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 264: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This implementation fails to visually hide the content, causing screen-reader-only text to appear on the screen for all users.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions update CSS classes and scroll locking but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line 1059: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but does not update the aria-pressed state on the trigger buttons. This prevents users of assistive technology from perceiving the toggle state of the menu buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1195: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.PRESSED, true);` Line 1204: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.PRESSED, false);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu initialization script registers triggers and panels but does not explicitly set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the buttons. Assistive technologies may not correctly announce the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1285: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The sticky header hide and show functions emit custom events and change CSS classes but do not provide a non-visual announcement. Users with visual impairments may not realize the header has been hidden or revealed during scroll.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: `liveRegion.announce('Header hidden');` Line 1363: `liveRegion.announce('Header visible');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator's _renderResult function updates the error message text and visibility but does not set the aria-invalid attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2905: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The clearField function in the inline validator removes error classes and hides the error element but fails to reset or remove the aria-invalid attribute. A previously invalid field will continue to be announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2945: `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions update CSS classes and scroll locking but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not receive confirmation that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line 1063: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view-mode toggle buttons (grid/list) update CSS classes and local storage but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which mode is active. This prevents screen reader users from perceiving the current state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2405: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true'); (and set 'false' on the inactive sibling)`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product grid view mode is toggled between grid and list, the visual layout changes but no announcement is made to the screen reader. Users may be unaware that the content structure has changed.

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inline validator updates the error message text and CSS classes but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Assistive technologies will not programmatically perceive the field as being in an error state.

issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation links use a `nav-link--current` class to visually indicate the active page but do not use `aria-current="page"` to programmatically identify the current link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1048: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The search icon (🔍) inside the search submit button is decorative as the button already has an `srLabel`, but the icon is not hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1218: `🔍`

issuesFound.title: toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation dropdown trigger uses the `isOpen` state to show/hide the sub-menu but does not communicate this state to assistive technologies. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1014: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The background video pause/play button uses the `isPlaying` state to toggle functionality but does not communicate this state via `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1345: `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-pressed={!isPlaying} aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input has a visual error state when `email.state.error` is present, but it does not use `aria-invalid` to programmatically communicate the error to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1855: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: loading button missing aria-busy

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator, such as box-shadow or border. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance, making it difficult for keyboard users to see which element has focus, affecting those with visual or motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: In the :focus-visible rule, change "outline: none;" to "outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);"

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm has min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum recommended for touch targets. This makes it difficult to activate for users with limited dexterity or on touch devices, violating WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size.
issuesFound.fix: In the .btn-sm rule, change "min-height: 20px;" to "min-height: 24px;"

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility sets properties like position: static and clip: auto, which do not hide content visually. This means content intended for screen readers remains visible, causing confusion and duplicate information for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Replace the .sr-only rule with: ".sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }"

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has transform and transition animations on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for it is empty, lacking an override. This can cause discomfort for users with motion sensitivity, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: In the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img, add: ".hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; }" and ".hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }"

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability in body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which are negative values that can compress text and reduce readability, especially for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: In the body rule, change "word-spacing: -0.2em;" to "word-spacing: normal;" and "letter-spacing: -0.05em;" to "letter-spacing: normal;"

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The contrast between the text colour (white, #ffffff) and background colour (#5588bb) is approximately 1.89:1, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum of 4.5:1 for normal text. This makes the text hard to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: In the .btn-primary rule, change "background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary);" to "background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);" to increase contrast.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: All links have underlines removed by default, and there is no consistent alternative visual indicator, such as underlines on hover or increased font-weight. This makes links difficult to distinguish from surrounding text for colour-blind users or those with low vision, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Change "a { text-decoration: none; }" to "a { text-decoration: underline; }" to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ffffff / background #ee9999 ratio 1.84:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class has a contrast ratio of 1.84:1 between the text and background, below the minimum 4.5:1 for normal text. This makes the text very difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: Darken the background or lighten the text to achieve a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground rgba(255,255,255,0.3) / background rgba(255,255,255,0.08) ratio too low
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label has very low contrast against the .stat-item background, making the text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 838: Increase the opacity of the text color to at least 0.87 (or use a solid color) to achieve a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against the background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 3.14:1
issuesFound.explanation: The link in the footer has a contrast ratio of 3.14:1 against the footer background, below the minimum 4.5:1 for normal text. This makes the link difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: Lighten the link color to achieve a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule removes underlines from all links, and there is no consistent alternative (such as underline on hover) to distinguish links from surrounding text. This makes it difficult for users with low vision or colour blindness to identify links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224: Remove text-decoration: none and let the browser default, or add a non-colour indicator such as an underline on hover for all links.

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px, height 28px
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card:hover
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded on initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set when the page loads with the menu closed. Screen reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav init function, after obtaining _trigger element, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons registered by dropdownMenu.register() do not have aria-expanded="false" set initially when their panels are hidden. Screen readers cannot know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu.register function, after setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and faqAccordion.close functions change visible state but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot know when answers expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open function, add: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In faqAccordion.close function, add: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel dynamic content updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When carousel slides change automatically or via navigation, the update is not announced to screen readers via an ARIA live region. Users cannot know when slide content changes.
issuesFound.fix: In testimonialsCarousel.goTo function, after updating current slide, add: liveRegion.announce(`Slide ${target + 1} of ${_slides.length}: ${_slides[target].label}`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields fail validation, the code adds CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen readers cannot detect invalid fields without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult function, when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); When valid, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions dynamic update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When search suggestions are fetched and rendered, the count of results is only updated in a visually hidden status element, but not announced via an assertive live region. Screen reader users may miss the update.
issuesFound.fix: In siteSearch._updateStatus function, after setting _statusEl.textContent, add: liveRegion.announceAssertive(_statusEl.textContent);

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button created by scrollToTop module does not have an aria-label attribute. Screen reader users will only hear "button" without knowing its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: In scrollToTop.init function, after obtaining _btn element, add: setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll to top');

issuesFound.title: Toast notification dismiss buttons missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: Toast dismiss buttons only have click event listeners. Keyboard users cannot activate them with Enter or Space keys, violating keyboard operability.
issuesFound.fix: In toastManager.show function, after creating dismissBtn, add: dismissBtn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); dismiss(toast); } });

fpTitles: Toast notification dismiss buttons missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle() function changes the visible state of the navigation drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid count and state but does not announce changes via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not notified when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3005: function applyFilters() { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied?.(s.activeCategory) || `Filter applied: ${s.activeCategory}`); }
        Line 3010: function applyFilters() { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied?.(s.activeCategory) || `Filter applied: ${s.activeCategory}`); }

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid inputs. Screen reader users are not informed of the validation error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3650: function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { ... setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', String(!result.valid)); }
        Line 3655: function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { ... setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', String(!result.valid)); }

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" on page load when the drawer is closed. Screen reader users start with incorrect state information.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1780: function init() { ... setExpanded(_trigger, false); }
        Line 1785: function init() { ... setExpanded(_trigger, false); }

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function does not clear aria-invalid when a field becomes valid. Screen reader users continue to hear invalid state after correction.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3660: function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { ... setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', String(!result.valid)); }
        Line 3665: function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { ... setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', String(!result.valid)); }

fpTitles: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: Search results highlighter uses innerHTML without ARIA live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The searchResultsHighlighter.highlight function updates visible content by injecting <mark> elements via innerHTML but does not announce this change to screen readers. Users who rely on assistive technology won't know that search terms have been highlighted in the results.
issuesFound.fix: After the highlighting is complete, add a live region announcement. Line 1536: Add `liveRegion.announce('Search terms highlighted in results');` after the `_highlightTokensInElement` call.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion._onContainerClick function toggles FAQ items but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users won't know whether an answer is expanded or collapsed, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the toggle function, update the trigger's aria-expanded attribute. Line 1088: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', !item.open);` before calling the toggle function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load when the drawer is closed. Screen reader users won't know the initial state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.init function, set the initial aria-expanded state. Line 1795: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the trigger element is found.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle button missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._onToggleClick function switches between monthly and annual billing but does not update aria-pressed on the toggle button. Screen reader users cannot tell which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Update the toggle button's aria-pressed attribute when the period changes. Line 1800: In _syncToggleState, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` after updating classes.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields as invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen reader users won't be notified of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Set aria-invalid when a field is invalid. Line 1088: In _renderResult, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', !result.valid);` inside the if/else blocks.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel auto-play without pause control for keyboard users
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel auto-plays slides but the pause/play button only has a click handler; keyboard users cannot activate it with Enter or Space. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Add a keydown handler to the play button. Line 1234: Add `_playBtn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); togglePlay(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button missing keyboard handler for non-button element
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop button is a div with role="button" and tabindex="0" but only has a click event listener. Keyboard users cannot activate it with Enter or Space, breaking WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Add a keydown handler for Enter and Space. Line 1795: Add `_btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onClick(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded="false" set initially when their panels are hidden. Screen reader users won't know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register, set the initial aria-expanded state. Line 1088: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications use innerHTML without ARIA live region for dynamic content
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager.show function creates toast messages using innerHTML but relies on the container's aria-live; however, the container is static. When toast content changes dynamically (e.g., different messages), there's no dedicated live region update.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each toast announcement is made via the liveRegion module. Line 1234: Add `liveRegion.announce(message);` after creating the toast element.

Scroll-to-top button missing keyboard handler for non-button element

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on generic :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) and does not supply any other visible focus styling (no box‑shadow, border or coloured outline). Keyboard‑only users therefore have no visual cue when an element receives focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }` →  
Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links. No other visual cue (border, background, font‑weight change) is provided for the base link state, so links are indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual differentiation beyond colour.

Line 120: `a { text-decoration: none; }` →

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-controls and aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) does not expose which element it controls and does not convey its expanded/collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-controls="ID_OF_DRAWER"` (where the drawer element has that ID) and set `aria-expanded="false"` initially. Update the attribute to `"true"` when the drawer opens and back to `"false"` when it closes (e.g., in the `open()` and `close()` functions).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria-expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The script toggles the drawer visibility but never updates the `aria-expanded` state on the toggle button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `open()` function call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in the `close()` function call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers receive `aria-controls` and `aria-haspopup` but lack `aria-expanded`, so their collapsed/expanded state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
When registering a dropdown, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. In `_openInstance()` set it to `"true"` and in `_closeInstance()` set it back to `"false"`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded attribute initially
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons get `aria-controls` but are not given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` when collapsed, causing a mismatch between visual state and accessibility state.
issuesFound.fix:
During initialization, for each FAQ item that is not open, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Ensure the attribute toggles to `"true"` when the item is opened.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing toggle missing aria-checked for switch role
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is given `role="switch"` but lacks the required `aria-checked` state, so its on/off status is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix:
Initialize with `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', 'false')`. In `_onToggleClick()` toggle the attribute between `"true"` and `"false"` to reflect the current period.

---

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Filter buttons act as toggles but only receive a CSS `active` class; they do not expose their pressed state via ARIA.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_syncTabAriaStates()`, after determining `isActive`, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` for each filter button.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing accessible name (aria-label)
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` is given role and ARIA attributes but no visible label, leaving it unnamed for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', 'Search site')` (or a more specific label) during initialization.

---

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The button (`#scroll-top-btn`) has no accessible name, making its purpose unclear to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
In `init()`, call `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', 'Scroll back to top')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel period tabs missing ARIA role and aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: Period navigation buttons are treated as tabs but lack `role="tab"` and `aria-selected`, so their selection state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
When creating period buttons, add `role="tab"` and `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', btn === activeBtn ? 'true' : 'false')`. Update the attribute on navigation changes.

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard tabs missing aria-controls and aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: Wizard step tabs are focusable but lack `role="tab"`, `aria-controls` linking to their panels, and `aria-selected` to indicate the active step.
issuesFound.fix: Assign each tab `role="tab"`, `aria-controls="ID_OF_PANEL"`, and toggle `aria-selected` between `"true"` and `"false"` as the user moves between steps. Ensure panels have `role="tabpanel"` and `aria-labelledby` referencing the tab.

fpTitles: Site‑search input missing accessible name (aria-label)
Scroll‑to‑top button missing aria-label

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.init() the trigger element is cached but never given an initial aria‑expanded="false". Screen readers will read an undefined state before the first interaction.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria‑expanded on their trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() show or hide the panel but never modify the aria‑expanded attribute on the associated trigger <button [data‑nav‑dropdown‑trigger]>. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.init() the code registers each trigger but does not set aria‑expanded="false". The initial accessibility state is therefore missing.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module’s _ onToggleClick() function toggles the billing period but never updates aria‑pressed on the [data‑billing‑toggle] element, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle lacks initial aria‑pressed state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In pricing.init() the toggle element is identified but never given an initial aria‑pressed value, so its state is ambiguous to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge() updates the visible count but does not send an announcement via a live region, so users of assistive technology are not informed when the number of visible products changes.

issuesFound.title: Pricing savings message updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: pricing._updateSavingsMessage() shows or hides a savings callout but does not use a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the discount information.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` is given `role="combobox"` and `aria-controls`, but never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute to indicate whether the suggestion listbox is open. Screen‑reader users cannot know the popup state.
issuesFound.fix:
After mounting the listbox, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and toggle it to `"true"` when the suggestions panel opens and back to `"false"` when it closes.

---

issuesFound.title: Suggestion list does not update aria‑selected on active option
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex()` toggles a CSS class on the active `<li role="option">` but never updates the required `aria‑selected` attribute, so assistive technologies cannot identify the highlighted suggestion.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the loop, after `toggleClass(item, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(item, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not manage aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed, the script adds/removes CSS classes but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility state undefined.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) controls the off‑canvas drawer but never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute, so users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_close()` set it to `'false'`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter category tabs do not reflect state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab `<button>` elements receive a visual “active” class, but the required `aria‑pressed` attribute is never updated, breaking the ARIA button‑toggle pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_syncTabAriaStates()` after toggling the CSS class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons and toggle switch do not update aria‑pressed / aria‑checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) and period selection buttons (`_periodBtns`) change visual state but never update `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑checked` for a switch), leaving the state ambiguous to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick()`/`_syncToggleState()` set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));` (or `aria-checked` for a switch). In `_syncToggleState()` set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` for each period button.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack aria‑hidden when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is closed, the panel receives `hidden` but never `aria‑hidden="true"`. Hidden content may still be announced by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()` after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` also call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels do not remove aria‑hidden when expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the panel’s `hidden` attribute is removed, but any existing `aria‑hidden` remains, keeping the panel invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` also call `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.fix: Ensure headings follow a logical sequence. If the <h1> is correct, the next heading should be <h2>, then <h3>, etc. No code change needed if the hierarchy is intentional, but review is required.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` selector sets a `min-height` and `min-width` of `28px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24px (44×44px recommended). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1123: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` selector applies a `transform` animation on hover, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block for this selector (lines 1335-1338) is empty and does not disable or slow the animation. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by reducing the space between words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a` selector removes underlines (`text-decoration: none`) from links, and no alternative visual indicator (e.g., border-bottom, background highlight, or font-weight increase) is provided on `:hover` or `:focus`. Colour alone distinguishes links, violating WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 320: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add a non-colour indicator on hover/focus, e.g., `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule removes the default focus outline with outline: none but does not provide a visible alternative for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1024: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm rule sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) requirement of 24×24 px for interactive elements. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active rule applies a transform: translateY(1px) on activation, which is a motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AAA, but often treated as AA).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by making text harder to parse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The html rule sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended minimum of 16px for body text. This can impair readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 290: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown`, `_onPointerUp`, and pointer event listeners are attached to the carousel root element, but there is no matching `keydown` handler for `Enter` or `Space` keys. This means keyboard users cannot activate the carousel slides or controls, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in `pricing` updates the billing period label (e.g., "Monthly" → "Annual") but does not announce this change to screen readers via an ARIA live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages, as users who rely on screen readers are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6190: Add the following line after the price updates:
    ```javascript
    liveRegion.announce(
      period === 'month'
        ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'
        : STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%'
    );
    ```

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onTriggerClick` (lines 11234–11243) handles click events on dropdown triggers but does not include a corresponding keydown handler for keyboard users (Enter/Space). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) and 2.5.3 (Label in Name) because non-native interactive elements must be fully operable via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown` and `_onPointerUp` event handlers in the `testimonialsCarousel` module (lines 10235–10249) are attached to a non-native interactive element (`_rootEl`). These handlers manage swipe gestures for carousel navigation but do not include corresponding keyboard event handlers (`keydown` or `keyup`) for Enter or Space keys. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe functionality, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard Accessible).

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the NavDropdown trigger button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the arrow characters, which is redundant since the button's accessible name already conveys its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1302: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="nav-link__chevron">`

issuesFound.title: Toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown trigger button controls a dropdown menu but lacks the aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers cannot announce whether the dropdown is open or closed, preventing users from understanding the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1299: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The search input in the SearchInput component lacks a visible label. While it has an aria-label, visible labels are preferred for all users, including those with cognitive disabilities who benefit from persistent visual cues.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1150: Remove the VisuallyHidden component around the label text and ensure the label is visible:
```
      <label id={labelId} htmlFor={id} className="site-search-form__label">
        Search Luminary Studio
      </label>
```

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component supports an invalid state but does not expose it to assistive technology via aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot announce when a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: `<input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The BillingToggle buttons use visual styling to indicate the active state but do not use aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology. Screen readers cannot announce which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1920: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`
  Fix: Line 1925: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required-field marker (*) in the FormGroup component is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce "asterisk", which is redundant since the field is already marked as required via the required attribute and the visually hidden "(required)" text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component supports a loading state but does not expose it to assistive technology via aria-busy. Screen readers cannot announce when a button is in a loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 780: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Spinner icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner icon inside the Button component during loading is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may attempt to announce the spinner, which is decorative and redundant since the loading state is already announced via the VisuallyHidden text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component highlights the current page link visually but does not use aria-current="page" to communicate this state to assistive technology. Screen readers cannot announce which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Required field not programmatically marked as required
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for "Full name" in the ContactForm is marked as required visually but lacks the required attribute or aria-required. Assistive technologies cannot announce that the field is required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2350: `<TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for "Full name" in the ContactForm renders an error message but does not link it to the input via aria-describedby. Screen readers may not associate the error message with the field.
issuesFound.fix:
The FormGroup component already handles this correctly, but ensure the errorId prop is passed:

issuesFound.title: Required field marked visually only — add aria-required or required attr

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a replacement visible focus style (such as border, box-shadow, or outline-color/width). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not have a more specific override, making the site unusable for keyboard-only users.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none but does not provide a visible alternative (border or box-shadow). Since this selector is more specific than the global rule, it actively hides focus on buttons, blocking keyboard access to primary actions.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (Icon Button)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum requirement of 24px (SC 2.5.8). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (Checkbox)
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] rule sets width and height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. This reduces the clickable area, increasing the risk of missed inputs for users with dexterity issues.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector applies word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative spacing can cause characters and words to overlap or become indistinct, significantly degrading readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities (SC 1.4.12).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform transition (line 595), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (line 608) is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the sliding animation, which may cause vestibular discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none. This suppresses the high-contrast outline that the user agent would otherwise force, potentially making focus invisible in High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative (Footer/Social)
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer and social-links section rely solely on color change to indicate hover/focus state (text-decoration: none is inherited globally). Color alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users with color vision deficiencies (SC 1.4.1).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple selectors set `outline: none` on `:focus-visible` without defining a replacement `outline-color`, `outline-style`, `outline-width`, `box-shadow`, or `border`. This removes the visible focus indicator for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which does not visually hide the element. Content intended for screen readers only will be visible on the page, cluttering the visual interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`. Negative word-spacing can cause words to overlap or become illegible, especially for users with cognitive disabilities or when text is zoomed, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 155: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector sets `text-decoration: none`. Links in body text are distinguished by color alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 175: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (24px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` and `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]` have explicit dimensions of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px. This makes activation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 595: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }
  Line 1425: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (44px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn`, `.nav-toggle`, and `#site-search button[type="submit"]` have heights between 28px and 32px. While above the 24px minimum, they fall short of the 44px recommended size for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8 AAA / 2.2 Guidelines), risking usability on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` blocks at lines 420 and 825 are empty or do not disable transitions (e.g., `.skip-link`, `.hero-image img`). Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience spatial animations, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 420: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }
  Line 825: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.explanation: `.section-header p` uses `--colour-text-secondary` (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.8:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast (Footer Bottom)
issuesFound.explanation: `.footer-bottom > p` uses `--colour-neutral-500` (#64748b) on `--colour-neutral-900` (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.4:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement (WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1495: .footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast (Stats Label)
issuesFound.explanation: `.stat-label` uses `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3)` on a colored gradient background. The contrast is extremely low (well below 3:1), making the text illegible for many users (WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Additionally, the `init()` function does not initialise this attribute. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu state change.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module do not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. The `register()` function also fails to initialise this attribute. This prevents assistive technologies from tracking the dropdown state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where the attribute is never set to true dynamically.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle function does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid()` and `markValid()` functions in `srUtils` (used by `inlineValidator`) update visual classes and `aria-describedby` but do not toggle the `aria-invalid` attribute on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border-color. This removes focus indicators for all interactive elements that lack their own specific focus styles, making keyboard navigation invisible to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a high-contrast alternative like outline: 2px solid ButtonText. Users in Windows High Contrast mode will lose all focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline but does not define a replacement visual focus indicator. Combined with the global outline removal, buttons show no visible focus state for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This mobile menu button will not show focus to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a replacement visual indicator. Links within dropdown menus will not show focus to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Search button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a replacement visual indicator. The search submit button will not show focus to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a replacement visual indicator. The expandable FAQ buttons will not show focus to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This creates a barrier for users with motor impairments who need larger activation areas.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28px touch target. This is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended touch target size for links. Increasing the target size would benefit users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which makes the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility class.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only focusable utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only-focusable class uses position: static and width: auto, making content visible instead of visually hidden. This prevents the utility from functioning as a skip-link or visually-hidden-focusable pattern.

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes transition: transform var(--transition-base) which causes spatial movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transition: transform var(--transition-fast) which causes spatial movement on activation, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img class includes transition: transform var(--transition-slower) which creates spatial movement on hover, but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 800 is empty and does not disable the transition.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at certain zoom levels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 324: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.fix: Line 866: .step-number { color: #ffffff; text-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); } or darken the background.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally, removing underlines from all links. Without an additional non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or background highlight) for links in body text, users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 331: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add a { border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates visually via `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches)` but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users are not informed that filtering has completed or how many results are available, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: After the line `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing module price changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, the price display updates visually and the aria-label is updated, but the change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize the price has changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._updatePlanCard: After the line `plan.priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-label', target);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target}`);`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel missing pause on tab visibility change
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel auto-plays but does not pause when the user switches to a different browser tab (document.hidden). The content continues to update while not visible, violating WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide).

fpTitles: testimonialsCarousel missing pause on tab visibility change

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobileNav module initializes, it selects the trigger button but does not set aria-expanded="false" to indicate the initial closed state of the navigation drawer. This violates the requirement to initialize ARIA states on page load for toggle widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160 (after _trigger assignment): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` to initialize the attribute.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register() function ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set on the trigger, but it does not initialize aria-expanded="false" when the menu is hidden on page load. This leaves the trigger without a defined expanded state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2263 (after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` to initialize the toggle state.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/yearly/quarterly) indicate the active state via CSS classes, but the code does not update aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers. These buttons function as toggle buttons and require aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4174: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop where isActive is determined.

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields fail validation, srUtils.markInvalid associates the error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Conversely, markValid does not clear aria-invalid. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying invalid fields programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4702 (in markInvalid): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after addDescribedBy. Line 4715 (in markValid): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`) after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the _updateCountBadge function updates the textContent and aria-label of the count badge, but because the element is not a live region and the update does not use the liveRegion announcer, screen reader users are not notified of the change in result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3827 (end of _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` to announce the count change.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel init function conditionally calls play() which sets aria-pressed, but when autoPlay is disabled, the play/pause button is never initialized with aria-pressed="false". This leaves the toggle button without a defined state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4475 (after selecting _playBtn): Add `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));` to initialize the pressed state regardless of auto-play setting.

issuesFound.title: Pricing card price changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the price text updates visually and the aria-label is updated, but without a live region announcement or aria-live on the element, screen reader users are not informed of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4145 (after updating priceDisplay textContent): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target}`);` or similar announcement to inform users of the change.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in the mobile navigation controller add and remove CSS classes to show/hide the drawer, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button to reflect the state change. Screen readers will not announce whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `open()` function after `_open = true;`. Line 2165: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the `close()` function after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions manage the visibility of dropdown panels but fail to toggle `aria-expanded` on the trigger buttons. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing when a dropdown menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2270: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance()` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2285: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `_closeInstance()` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in the FAQ accordion animate the panels and update internal state, but they do not synchronize the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger buttons. Although initial state is set in `init()`, subsequent toggles are not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3740: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `open()` after `item.open = true;`. Line 3770: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `close()` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox `_open()` and `_close()` functions manage the suggestion panel visibility but do not update `aria-expanded` on the input element. The input has `role="combobox"` and requires `aria-expanded` to indicate whether the listbox popup is displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3090: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_open()`. Line 3100: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `_close()`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState()` function updates the visual active state of billing period buttons via CSS classes but does not set `aria-pressed` to indicate the pressed state to screen readers. These buttons act as a toggle group where one is always pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3390: Inside the `_periodBtns.forEach` loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates()` function toggles active CSS classes on filter tab buttons but omits `aria-pressed`. Since these buttons toggle the filter state and remain pressed when active, they require `aria-pressed` to communicate their state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2940: Inside the `forEach` loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod()` function dynamically updates price values and billing labels when the user switches between monthly and annual billing, but it does not announce these changes to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3350: At the end of `setPeriod()`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or corresponding message for the selected period).

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button click not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onClick()` handler for the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page and moves focus, but it does not announce the action completion via a live region. While focus management is present, an explicit announcement confirms the action for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` inside `_onClick()` after the scroll operation.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult()` function in the inline validator marks fields as valid or invalid by adding/removing CSS classes and updating `aria-describedby`, but it never sets `aria-invalid` to `'true'` on invalid fields or `'false'` on valid fields. Screen readers rely on this attribute to indicate invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4190: In the `if (result.valid)` block, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`. In the `else` block, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` function in siteSearch configures the input as a combobox with `aria-controls` and `aria-haspopup`, but it does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` to indicate that the suggestion listbox is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3140: After setting up the input attributes, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu toggle button does not communicate its expanded state. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1250: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>
