issuesFound.fix: Line 601-604: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when a "Learn more" link has focus (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 718-721: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which filter button has focus (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 819-821: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which billing toggle has focus (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 918-920: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the table region has focus for scrolling (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1048-1051: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset: -3px, which does not render a visible indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which FAQ question has focus (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1134-1135: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline on focus-visible. Although :focus has a box-shadow, browsers that support :focus-visible apply those styles instead of :focus for keyboard navigation, meaning the box-shadow may not render (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185-1187: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 0; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which checkbox has focus (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1193-1194: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when footer links have focus (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1208-1210: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which footer navigation link has focus (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1214-1217: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which social link has focus (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1221-1224: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: In Windows High Contrast mode, the :focus-visible rule inside @media (forced-colors: active) removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Users in high contrast mode cannot see focus indicators (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 257-260: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments or using touch devices may have difficulty activating small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447-451: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Icon-only buttons are particularly challenging for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 458-462: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are sized at 20×20px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments or using touch devices may have difficulty checking boxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1191-1192: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Base font size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which becomes the root for all rem units. This results in body text at 12px (below the recommended 16px minimum), impairing readability for users with low vision or reading difficulties (WCAG 1.4.4).
issuesFound.fix: Line 83: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words by 20%. This compresses text and impairs readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision (WCAG 1.4.8, 1.4.12).
issuesFound.fix: Line 95: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces the space between characters by 5%. This compresses text and impairs readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision (WCAG 1.4.8, 1.4.12).
issuesFound.fix: Line 96: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraphs have line-height: 1, which is below the recommended 1.5 minimum for body text. Insufficient line height impairs readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision (WCAG 1.4.8, 1.4.12).
issuesFound.fix: Line 108: p { line-height: var(--line-height-normal); text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non-color alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links globally. Links in body text are distinguished from surrounding text by color alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 111: a { text-decoration: underline; } and add a contextual exception: .nav-menu a, .footer-nav a, [role="navigation"] a { text-decoration: none; }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Negative letter-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not set initial aria-expanded="false" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav trigger button does not receive an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute when the module initializes. Assistive technology users may not know the current state of the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2005: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter._updateCountBadge() does not announce count changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product count badge updates after filtering, the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users cannot perceive that the number of visible products has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4154: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count) || \`Showing \${count} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggle changes (monthly/annual), the price updates are visual only. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4715: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips.render() does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed, the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users cannot perceive which filters are currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4275: add `liveRegion.announce(\`Filter \${label} \${value ? 'removed' : 'applied'}\`);` inside the chip creation loop, or add a summary announcement after chips are rendered

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. Screen readers cannot announce the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2538: function register(trigger, panel) { if (!isElement(trigger) || !isElement(panel)) return; if (_getInstance(trigger)) return; if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-haspopup')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true'); const panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel'); if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId); if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); ... }

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState() does not set aria-pressed on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons receive CSS class updates but aria-pressed is never set. Toggle buttons require aria-pressed to communicate their on/off state to screen reader users per WCAG 2.1/2.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3118: function _syncToggleState(period) { if (!_periodBtns) return; _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const btnPeriod = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); const isActive = btnPeriod === period; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); }); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error message from aria-describedby but never clears aria-invalid. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1623: function markValid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field)) return; field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID); if (isElement(errorEl) && errorEl.id) { const refs = (field.getAttribute('aria-describedby') || '').split(/\s+/).filter(id => id && id !== errorEl.id); if (refs.length) { field.setAttribute('aria-describedby', refs.join(' ')); } else { field.removeAttribute('aria-describedby'); } } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close function removes CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the hamburger button trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2261: function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not move focus into the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button and is not moved into the drawer. Keyboard users must manually Tab into the opened menu, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order. Focus should move to the first focusable element in the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2241: function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen); if (_overlay) { _overlay.removeAttribute('hidden'); requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(_overlay, CSS.visible)); } _lockScroll(); requestAnimationFrame(() => { const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus(); }); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer }); }

'all' : category; liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(categoryName) : `Showing ${categoryName} products`); emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', { count: totalMatches, state: Object.assign({}, s) }); }); }

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggle changes between monthly and annual, the visual price updates but screen reader users are not notified of the change. A live region announcement should communicate the new billing period.

(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing')); emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period }); }

issuesFound.title: suggestionListRenderer.mount() does not ensure search input has accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is enhanced with aria-autocomplete, aria-controls, role="combobox", and aria-haspopup but no aria-label or aria-labelledby is added. If the HTML input lacks an associated label, screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2696: function mount(input) { if (_listEl) return _listEl; _inputEl = input; _listId = srUtils.ensureId(input, 'search-input') + '-listbox'; if (!input.getAttribute('aria-label') && !input.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) { setAttr(input, 'aria-label', 'Search'); } _listEl = createElement('ul', { id : _listId, role : 'listbox', class : 'suggestion-list', 'aria-label' : STRINGS.searchSuggestionsLabel || 'Search suggestions', hidden : '', }); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button during initialization. The initial state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2772: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function sets item.open to true and adds CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetry where opening the accordion leaves the aria state stale.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4216: add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function only adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message, but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot programmatically identify that the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1848: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Fields that were previously invalid retain the stale aria-invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1863: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` inside the else branch after `field.removeAttribute('aria-describedby');`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid state for error communication
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop but only uses it for CSS class styling. When a field has a validation error, assistive technology users are not notified because aria-invalid="true" is never set on the input element.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid state for error communication
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an `invalid` prop but only uses it for CSS class styling. When a field has a validation error, assistive technology users are not notified because aria-invalid="true" is never set on the textarea element.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid state for error communication
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an `invalid` prop but only uses it for CSS class styling. When a field has a validation error, assistive technology users are not notified because aria-invalid="true" is never set on the select element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav hamburger toggle button missing aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has aria-controls and aria-label, but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine whether the mobile navigation panel is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing required aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. The LABELS object is defined but never used. Icon-only buttons must have an accessible name for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous button IconButton passes aria-controls but not aria-label. Icon-only buttons require an accessible name. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1468-1471:
```tsx
          <IconButton
            aria-label="Previous testimonial"
            aria-controls={trackId}
            onClick={handlePrev}
            className="testimonials-track__prev"
          >
            ‹
          </IconButton>
```

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next button IconButton passes aria-controls but not aria-label. Icon-only buttons require an accessible name. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1472-1475:
```tsx
          <IconButton
            aria-label="Next testimonial"
            aria-controls={trackId}
            onClick={handleNext}
            className="testimonials-track__next"
          >
            ›
          </IconButton>
```

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs category buttons missing aria-pressed state attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate selection through CSS classes only. The `activeFilter` state determines which filter is active, but this state is not communicated to assistive technology via aria-pressed.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed state attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle buttons indicate selection through CSS classes only. The `value` state determines which billing period is selected, but this state is not communicated to assistive technology via aria-pressed.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When a button is in the loading state (isLoading=true), assistive technology users are not notified that the button is busy. The aria-busy attribute should be set to communicate the loading state programmatically.

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner span inside the loading button is visible to assistive technology. Since there is already a VisuallyHidden loading announcement, the decorative spinner should be hidden from screen readers with aria-hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix:
Line 508-511:
```tsx
      {isLoading && (
        <>
          <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />
          <VisuallyHidden>{loadingLabel}</VisuallyHidden>
        </>
      )}
```

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field asterisk missing aria-hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The required field marker asterisk (*) is announced by screen readers as "asterisk" or "star", creating redundant or confusing output. Since a VisuallyHidden "(required)" label is already provided, the visual asterisk should be hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 546-552: ```tsx {required && ( <> * (required) )} ```

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron arrow characters (▲/▼) inside the dropdown trigger button are decorative since the button already has text content. These characters should be hidden from screen readers to prevent redundant announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 832-834: ```tsx {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'} ```

issuesFound.title: StarRating star characters missing aria-hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The star characters (★/☆) inside the rating component are decorative since the parent span already has an aria-label providing the accessible name. These characters should be hidden from screen readers to prevent redundant announcement.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badge star character missing aria-hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The star character (★) inside the trust badge is decorative since the span already has an aria-label. The character should be hidden from screen readers to prevent it being announced in addition to the aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1043-1046: ```tsx ★ 4.9 on G2 ```

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links with `item.current` set to true use a CSS class to indicate the current page visually, but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links with `item.current` set to true use a CSS class to indicate the current page visually, but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection missing aria-labelledby for landmark accessible name

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby for landmark accessible name

issuesFound.title: IconButton in ThemeToggle missing required aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop for accessibility, but ThemeToggle does not pass one. Screen reader users will hear only the icon character (☀️, 🌙, or 💻) without context, making the button's purpose unclear.

issuesFound.title: Modal has aria-modal on overlay container instead of dialog element

issuesFound.title: StatItem value lacks accessible name for formatted numbers

The Stat interface includes valueLabel for this purpose, but it is not used in the rendering. Screen readers may announce "40,000+" literally rather than "40,000 plus".

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible lack aria-hidden="true", which may cause screen readers to announce all slides simultaneously or navigate to hidden content. Only the visible slide should be accessible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list for marquee loop missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input has broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the <label> element. This breaks the programmatic association between labels and their corresponding form inputs, violating WCAG 1.3.1 and 4.1.2. Screen reader users cannot identify which input each label describes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 683: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slides missing aria-hidden when not visible
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible carousel slides lack aria-hidden="true", causing all slides to be visible to assistive technology simultaneously. Screen reader users will hear all testimonials at once rather than just the currently visible one, creating a confusing experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1078: aria-hidden={!isVisible}

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". Per ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the dialog element itself. This may cause screen readers to incorrectly announce modal behavior.
